model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Keep the successful LR schedule but switch to AdamW with moderately stronger decoupled weight decay, which often improves short-budget MLP generalization on CIFAR-like tasks.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP width to raise representational capacity while keeping the training loop and optimization setup unchanged, targeting lower validation loss within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Revert the width increase that regressed validation loss, restoring the better-performing smaller MLP while keeping the successful optimizer and scheduler settings.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.05 * total_steps))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Shorten warmup to allocate more of the 256-step budget to higher effective learning rates, which can improve convergence speed in this fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.05 * total_steps))","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Undo the shorter warmup change that hurt validation loss and return to the previously better warmup schedule.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Add mild label smoothing to improve generalization and calibration for the fixed-step MLP training regime with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Remove label smoothing because it significantly worsened validation loss in this setup, restoring the prior better-performing objective.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Test a slightly lower peak learning rate to reduce late-step over-updating while preserving the same warmup+cosine schedule and optimizer.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Restore the previous learning rate since lowering it degraded validation loss under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increase batch size under fixed-step evaluation so each optimization step uses more examples, which can improve loss within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Since increasing batch size to 128 produced a large gain under fixed-step evaluation, test 256 to further increase sample throughput per optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue the successful scaling trend by increasing batch size to 512 so each fixed training step incorporates more data.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has consistently improved fixed-step loss so far; test 1024 to further increase data processed per optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Push the same high-impact lever further by doubling batch size again, maximizing examples per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue exploiting the strongest observed trend by increasing batch size again, aiming to maximize useful data per fixed training step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling continues to deliver consistent gains, so test 8192 to push sample coverage per fixed step even further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With much larger batches, a higher learning rate can better utilize each step and may improve convergence within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With larger batch and higher LR now in place, slightly reducing weight decay may lower underfitting and improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Use the final step to probe a slightly more aggressive learning-rate scale, which can still help with very large-batch fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
